definition: Use molecular fingerprints and a Tanimoto-aware candidate index, tree, bit-bound pruning rule, or MinHash LSH sketch to avoid evaluating every molecule pair. Exact Tanimoto can be used for reranking emitted neighbors.

scale_profile.best_for: Large molecule libraries represented by sparse or bit-vector fingerprints.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Millions of molecules with appropriate fingerprint and index choices.
scale_profile.avoid_when: The molecular similarity definition is not fingerprint-based or when scaffold-level audit axes are required instead.

tunable_parameters.name: fingerprint_type
tunable_parameters.purpose: Defines the molecular feature space, for example Morgan, ECFP, or MHFP.

tunable_parameters.name: lsh_or_tree_parameters
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls candidate retrieval speed and recall.

quality_gates: Compute exact Tanimoto for final emitted candidates.
Benchmark candidate recall on an exact subset.
Report fingerprint radius, bit length, and collision-sensitive choices.
failure_modes: Fingerprint collisions can make unrelated molecules look similar.
A top-k fingerprint graph may not control scaffold leakage by itself.
LSH settings can miss moderately similar compounds near the audit threshold.
example_usage: python bitset_tanimoto_topk.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --fingerprint-col fingerprint --top-k 50

citations.title: A tree-based method for rapid screening of chemical fingerprints
citations.evidence: Uses tree and bound-based ideas to reduce explicit Tanimoto computations for large chemical databases.

citations.title: MHFP6: a molecular fingerprint for fast, scalable chemical similarity search
citations.evidence: Combines molecular fingerprints with MinHash/LSH to enable faster approximate nearest-neighbor search over compounds.
